meta.source: Foundry PF2e bestiary packs + curated seed
meta.notes: Augmented from core Foundry monster packs (max 800).

creatures.name: Animated Broom
creatures.description: Animated brooms perform menial tasks of cleaning and upkeep, but they can step in to defend a room from intrusion if needed.

creatures.name: Clockwork Spy
creatures.description: Engineers, technologists, and mechanically gifted wizards employ clockwork spies-tiny, spiderlike constructs capable of recording and playing back audio-to surreptitiously surveil their enemies or steal secrets from competitors.

creatures.name: Compsognathus
creatures.description: The compsognathus is a small bipedal dinosaur that moves in swift, darting motions.

creatures.name: Crawling Hand
creatures.description: A crawling hand formed from the appendage of a Medium creature is quick and agile, skittering in the shadows until it can strike its prey.

creatures.name: Eagle
creatures.description: A keen-eyed raptor soaring on wide wings.

creatures.name: Flash Beetle
creatures.description: These 3-foot-long insects boast a pair of glowing organs on the back of the abdomen that give off bright light and that continue to glow for days, even after the creature's death.

creatures.name: Giant Centipede
creatures.description: A segmented insectoid the length of a human arm.

creatures.name: Giant Rat
creatures.description: An oversized rat the size of a small dog.

creatures.name: Goblin Warrior
creatures.description: A small, green-skinned goblin brandishing a dogslicer.

creatures.name: Grimple
creatures.description: Even more than most gremlins, grimples resent the trappings of civilization: inn common rooms with their rowdy singalongs, livery yards with their whinnying horses, church steeples with their clanging bells.

creatures.name: Guard Dog
creatures.description: The typical guard dog is loyal and beloved by many communities.

creatures.name: Gutter Ooze
creatures.description: These small masses of protoplasm are considered the lesser "cousins" to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Sewer Ooze]{sewer oozes}, emerging from the drainage culverts beneath a city after a rainfall to feed on bits of organic matter that collect in streetside gutters.

creatures.name: Halfling Street Watcher
creatures.description: Halflings' keen sight makes them excellent street watchers.

creatures.name: Haniver
creatures.description: Hanivers are the most benign type of gremlin—capricious fey who skim the waves on rubbery fin-wings.

creatures.name: Kobold Warrior
creatures.description: A small, scaled kobold clutching a spear.

creatures.name: Mitflit
creatures.description: Mitflits are self-loathing and pitiful cowards, easily bullied into servitude by other creatures or even slightly more powerful mitflit leaders.

creatures.name: Skeleton Guard
creatures.description: An animated skeleton wielding ancient weapons.

creatures.name: Sprigjack
creatures.description: Weaker twigjacks are often found in cultivated forests and even gardens.

creatures.name: Sprite
creatures.description: Common sprites, sometimes called firefly sprites, are primeval guardians that latch onto a person, place, or object and defend it for their own inscrutable reasons.

creatures.name: Tooth Fairy
creatures.description: Lone fairies usually need several minutes of elbow grease and a sleeping or restrained subject to extract a tooth.

creatures.name: Viper
creatures.description: Each member of this family of venomous snakes has long, hinged fangs that inject potent venom into their prey.

creatures.name: Zombie Shambler
creatures.description: A shambling corpse animated by dark magic.

creatures.name: Air Wisp
creatures.description: Air wisps are floating spheres of cloud and storm, perpetually humming a light, whispery tone.

creatures.name: Azarketi Crab Catcher
creatures.description: The average azarketi citizen in Absalom makes a living fishing or catching crabs.

creatures.name: Badger
creatures.description: The typical badger has dark, brownish-gray fur highlighted with white markings, particularly on the head, giving it a striped mask of fur around its eyes. A threatened badger can swiftly become a ferocious combatant that typically fights until slain.

creatures.name: Bottlenose Dolphin
creatures.description: The bottlenose dolphin is the most common and widespread species of dolphin.

creatures.name: Dream Spider
creatures.description: A dream spider's webs have an iridescent hue and are infused with the same hallucinogenic compound as the creature's toxin.

creatures.name: Earth Wisp
creatures.description: Earth wisps are rolling spheres of rock, mud, and leaves, perpetually humming with low, rumbling energy.

creatures.name: Fire Wisp
creatures.description: Fire wisps are sizzling spheres of flame and ash, perpetually crackling and shimmering with heat.

creatures.name: Giant Maggot
creatures.description: Giant flies lay their eggs on the bodies of larger monsters or livestock.

creatures.name: Grindylow
creatures.description: The top half of a grindylow vaguely resembles a goblin, but from the waist down, their bodies split into a tangle of suckered, wriggling tentacles.

creatures.name: Homunculus
creatures.description: A homunculus is a tiny servitor construct created by a crafter to serve as a spy, scout, messenger, or assistant.

creatures.name: Hryngar Sharpshooter
creatures.description: Deep beneath the surface, the dour dwarves known as hryngars stubbornly toil, claiming the ancestral subterranean homelands of other dwarves as their own.

creatures.name: Leaf Leshy
creatures.description: Leaf leshies are diminutive protectors of forests clad in armor and hats made of fruit, flowers, or leaves.

creatures.name: Orc Scrapper
creatures.description: Acting as the most common orc combat unit, orc scrappers are ferocious fighters who rely on unrelenting strength, rather than combat experience, to prove their mettle and attempt to rise in the hold's standing.

creatures.name: Ort
creatures.description: These shapeless masses of quivering flesh are the least of devilkind.

creatures.name: Pugwampi
creatures.description: Mean, dog-faced, and craven, pugwampis take disproportionate enjoyment from the accidents and missteps of other creatures—something that happens often due to the supernatural aura of ill fortune these gremlins project.

creatures.name: Riding Pony
creatures.description: Horses serve as mounts and beasts of burden in many societies.

creatures.name: Skeleton Archer
creatures.description: A skeleton with an unerring aim, guarding ancient crypts.

creatures.name: Soulbound Homunculus
creatures.description: Most homunculi use a dose of their creator's blood as their spark of life, but it's possible to use a technique similar to that used in the crafting of a soulbound doll to give a homunculus a personality and the semblance of life.

creatures.name: Spider Swarm
creatures.description: An abundance of food, the sudden hatching of a clutch of eggs, or magical influence can cause smaller spiders to gather in terrifying, deadly masses. Few everyday vermin inspire as much dread as the infamous spider.

creatures.name: Wolf
creatures.description: A grey wolf hunting in packs.

creatures.name: Air Scamp
creatures.description: A pale blue head and paper-thin wings peek out from the cloud that clings to the air scamp.

creatures.name: Amoeba Swarm
creatures.description: An amoeba swarm consists of thousands of individual single-celled organisms held together by acrid-smelling slime. As ravenous as they are mindless, amoeba swarms use no tactics.

creatures.name: Animated Silverware Swarm
creatures.description: Nobles are known to pay great amounts to animate their silverware, both for ease in cleaning and to serve as novelties during dinner parties. Many animated objects have useful functions but become dangers when uncontrolled.

creatures.name: Arbiter
creatures.description: These spherical aeons are scouts and diplomats.

creatures.name: Augur
creatures.description: These spherical knots of sinewy muscle, serrated blades, and bloody metal are the most common velstracs on the Netherworld.

creatures.name: Bandit
creatures.description: A common road bandit looking for easy marks.

creatures.name: Boggard Scout
creatures.description: Often tasked with patrolling the borders of their lands, boggard scouts learn to speak another language (typically Common) to deal with trespassers.

creatures.name: Brownie
creatures.description: Brownies make their homes in the trunks of hollow trees, small earthy burrows, and even under porches or within the crawl spaces of farmhouses.

creatures.name: Cacodaemon
creatures.description: These twisted embodiments of violence and spite are spawned from eddies of angry and warped souls amid Abaddon's mists.

creatures.name: Caligni Dancer
creatures.description: Caligni dancers serve as intermediaries between caligni factions, carrying messages and negotiating deals between the notoriously independent groups.

creatures.name: Camel
creatures.description: For generations untold, nomads and traders have relied on sure-footed camels to cross the harsh deserts and trackless wastes around the world.

creatures.name: Carbuncle
creatures.description: Never have legend and misinformation met upon a more inauspicious brow than that of the lowly carbuncle.

creatures.name: Cassisian
creatures.description: The weakest of angels, cassisians usually serve as lackey messengers for more powerful angels or as spiritual guides for mortals.

creatures.name: Catfolk Pouncer
creatures.description: Catfolk pouncers travel the world in search of new experiences.

creatures.name: Cunning Fox
creatures.description: Spirit guides of cunning are often seen as the weakest of the spirit guides, but they're also the most numerous and proactive, guiding families to safe paths or leaving food and water for warriors.

creatures.name: Cythnigot
creatures.description: The cythnigot is a foul fungal parasite that grows and thrives in the corpses of small creatures.

creatures.name: Deep One
creatures.description: An average mature deep one weighs 300 pounds and measures 7 feet in length, though they appear shorter on land due to their wide stance and natural hunch.

creatures.name: Deep One Hybrid
creatures.description: Whether from their devout faith to Dagon or their own biological lineage, some humans display physical indicators of their connection to deep ones.

creatures.name: Doru
creatures.description: These deceivers whisper fetid lies, sowing dark and dread inspiration into foolish mortal ears.

creatures.name: Drow Fighter
creatures.description: A drow warrior trained in the fighting styles of the Darklands.

creatures.name: Dwarf Warrior
creatures.description: Between their finely crafted equipment and natural bravery, a dwarf warrior can hold their ground against mighty foes.

creatures.name: Earth Scamp
creatures.description: An earth scamp's rocky exterior could camouflage it well among loose rocks if not for the scamp's large eyes and bat-like wings.

creatures.name: Electric Eel
creatures.description: Usually found in freshwater rivers and lakes, an electric eel is not particularly aggressive, but its ability to stun predators and prey alike can be dangerous to larger creatures searching for their next meal.

creatures.name: Elf Ranger
creatures.description: Many elves learn the bow before even picking up their first blade. Because of this, many elves act as rangers at some point in their long lives. Elves are mysterious and intelligent, and graceful and cunning in battle.

creatures.name: Esipil
creatures.description: Among the least of the sahkils, esipils delight in spreading fears and uncertainty among people who live with animals that could turn on them.

creatures.name: Festrog
creatures.description: Spawned from corpses of those who died of illness or starvation and were twisted by void energy, festrogs display a viciousness that rivals other undead.

creatures.name: Fetchling Scout
creatures.description: Fetchling scouts patrol the outskirts of their communities, looking for any possible threats.

creatures.name: Fire Mephit
creatures.description: A small fire elemental with a nasty temperament.

creatures.name: Fire Scamp
creatures.description: Although arguably quite friendly, fire scamps are considered far more dangerous than their kin.

creatures.name: Fuath
creatures.description: Despite their small size, fuaths are shipwreckers incarnate.

creatures.name: Ghoul
creatures.description: An undead creature that feeds on the flesh of corpses.

creatures.name: Ghoul Stalker
creatures.description: Ghouls stalkers are ravenous undead who haunt graveyards and eat corpses. Few creatures are more ubiquitous to sinister locations such as lonely graveyards and ruined crypts than the flesh-eating undead known as ghouls.

creatures.name: Giant Amoeba
creatures.description: These blobs of nearly transparent protoplasm are identical in form and behavior to the microscopic creatures from which they have evolved, except their outlandish size makes them all the more dangerous.

creatures.name: Giant Ant
creatures.description: A dog-sized ant that overwhelms prey in numbers.

creatures.name: Giant Cockroach
creatures.description: Cockroaches are communal creatures, rarely setting out on their own unless they are searching for food. If an adventurer encounters a single giant cockroach while exploring, they had best take precautions against others, as there is likely an entire colony nearby.

creatures.name: Giant Fly
creatures.description: This massive fly's twitching limbs seem to never stop moving as it constantly cleans itself and scoops anything edible into its mouthparts.

creatures.name: Giant Frog
creatures.description: Giant frogs can grow up to 6 feet long and weigh over 200 pounds, with rows of razor-sharp teeth lining their gaping mouths. Frogs that are poisonous or grow to monstrous size can be a menace to adventurers.

creatures.name: Giant Gecko
creatures.description: These roughly human-sized reptiles have amazing feet capable of clinging tenaciously to any surface.

creatures.name: Giant Spider
creatures.description: A spider the size of a pony with venomous fangs.

creatures.name: Gnome Bard
creatures.description: Being a bard allows a gnome a wonderful excuse to constantly travel and see new places while simultaneously creating wonderful things.

creatures.name: Gnome Scout
creatures.description: A small, quick gnome explorer and pathfinder.

creatures.name: Goblin Commando
creatures.description: A goblin warrior with better equipment and leadership ambitions.

creatures.name: Goblin Dog
creatures.description: Goblins' eponymous pets aren't true canines at all but rather large, blunt-nosed rodents with thin bodies and long legs.

creatures.name: Goblin Pyro
creatures.description: A goblin obsessed with fire magic and arson.

creatures.name: Goblin Snake
creatures.description: Goblin snakes sometimes train snakes as pets or companions. A goblin snake is a foul-smelling serpentine creature covered in greasy black scales and sporting a fanged, lipless head resembling that of a goblin.

creatures.name: Goblin War Chanter
creatures.description: While all goblins enjoy singing, goblin war chanters pride themselves on mastering the art of vocal performance.

creatures.name: Gourd Leshy
creatures.description: Gourd leshies are guardians of fields, gardens, and farms.

creatures.name: Grioth Scout
creatures.description: The first grioths encountered on a new world are scouts.

creatures.name: Guard
creatures.description: A town guard in chain mail armor keeping the peace.

creatures.name: Halfling Troublemaker
creatures.description: A halfling's bravado can sometimes lead them into trouble.

creatures.name: Hippocampus
creatures.description: Hippocampi serve as steeds for aquatic humanoids or roam wild in the sea.

creatures.name: Hobgoblin Soldier
creatures.description: A disciplined hobgoblin warrior in formation armor.

creatures.name: House Drake
creatures.description: Beautifully colored in purples and electric blues, the little dragonets called house drakes are genuinely brilliant in more ways than one.

creatures.name: Hryngar Bombardier
creatures.description: Hryngar chemists have a knack for creating alchemical grenades.

creatures.name: Hunting Spider
creatures.description: Hunting spiders are the most common type of giant spider, though not the largest. Few everyday vermin inspire as much dread as the infamous spider.

creatures.name: Hyena
creatures.description: Hyenas are cunning, powerfully built carnivores that bear a heavy resemblance to dogs and other canines, though they are not themselves canines.

creatures.name: Imp
creatures.description: Imps are fiendish infiltrators and corrupters who, despite their diminutive stature, are more than capable of subtly influencing a weak-willed individual into performing increasingly evil acts over time.

creatures.name: Jinkin
creatures.description: Jinkins are sadistic tinkers who steal and sabotage items and take great pride in their power to curse precious objects.

creatures.name: Kobold Scout
creatures.description: Most kobolds encountered outside of a well-defended warren or lair are kobold scouts, those trained for stalking and hunting.

creatures.name: Leopard
creatures.description: A sleek big cat stalking its prey.

creatures.name: Lizardfolk Defender
creatures.description: A lizardfolk defender serves as a protector of the young, guardian of the settlement, and when no other options are available, a soldier in times of war.

creatures.name: Lizardfolk Scout
creatures.description: Iruxi scouts are the lizardfolk most often encountered by adventurers, for these unsentimental explorers and hunters spend most of their lives on the move, constantly patrolling the territories held by their enclave.

creatures.name: Lyrakien
creatures.description: Lyrakiens are musical messengers and embodiments of free travel.

creatures.name: Merfolk Warrior
creatures.description: Merfolk warriors form the bulk of the militias of their vast underwater realms and meet potential aggressors head-on with uncompromising force.

creatures.name: Naari Pyrochemist
creatures.description: Naaris' blood blazes with elemental fire. On the Plane of Fire, most naaris are second-class citizens who serve under the iron heel of the ifrits and their Dominion of Flame, but those born outside the ifrit hierarchy or who choose to flee it live lives of passion in search of fame, glory, and powe…

creatures.name: Naiad
creatures.description: Naiads protect streams, ponds, springs, and other natural bodies of fresh water.

creatures.name: Nixie
creatures.description: These aquatic fey often guard ponds, rivers, lakes, and springs, protecting their bucolic homes from the advances of predators and careless settlers alike.

creatures.name: Nosoi
creatures.description: A nosoi resembles a whippoorwill, sparrow, or other small bird wearing a heavy leather plague doctor's mask.

creatures.name: Orc Veteran
creatures.description: Orc veterans have survived several bloody and chaotic conflicts, coming out the other side with scars and experience that make them even more dangerous opponents.

creatures.name: Orc Warrior
creatures.description: A fierce orc warrior wielding a massive greataxe.

creatures.name: Plague Zombie
creatures.description: Plague zombies are infested with horrible contagions. A zombie's only desire is to consume the living. Unthinking and ever-shambling harbingers of death, zombies stop only when they're destroyed.

creatures.name: Python
creatures.description: This nonvenomous family of snakes is rarely a threat to anything but small mammals and birds, hunting by coiling around prey and crushing victims with their powerful muscles.

creatures.name: Raktavarna
creatures.description: Raktavarnas are simple rakshasa incarnations of betrayal and assassination, often existing to test the mettle of those who were treacherous or poisonous in a previous life.

creatures.name: Rat Swarm
creatures.description: A swarm of rats can cause total chaos within a household or business.

creatures.name: Reefclaw
creatures.description: Reefclaws are aquatic monsters that resemble huge shrimp or lobsters.

creatures.name: Riding Dog
creatures.description: Riding dogs types include larger dogs, such as mastiffs, wolfhounds, and huskies, and are bred often by halflings and gnomes to serve as mounts.

creatures.name: Riding Horse
creatures.description: Horses serve as mounts and beasts of burden in many societies.

creatures.name: Sewer Ooze
creatures.description: These amorphous masses of sewage and other detritus make their way through filthy culverts beneath cities large and small. Slimes, molds, and other oozes can be found in dank dungeons and shadowed forests. While not necessarily evil, some grow to enormous sizes and have insatiable appetites.

creatures.name: Umbral Gnome Scout
creatures.description: Umbral gnome scouts patrol the tunnels that lead into their settlements.

creatures.name: Vampire Bat Swarm
creatures.description: Although the typical vampire bat has a wingspan of 7 inches and doesn't pose a significant threat to larger prey alone (and indeed, these blood-drinkers can feed without their sleeping victims ever noticing), some unusually aggressive species of these bats hunt in deadly swarms.

creatures.name: Velociraptor
creatures.description: A smaller cousin of the deinonychus, the velociraptor is a swift, cunning pack hunter.

creatures.name: Voidworm
creatures.description: Other proteans don't consider the flying, iridescent beings known as voidworms to be part of a protean caste at all, but instead merely a shameful side effect of the Maelstrom's constantly churning energy.

creatures.name: War Pony
creatures.description: Horses serve as mounts and beasts of burden in many societies.

creatures.name: Water Scamp
creatures.description: Water scamps are marked apart from other scamps by sleek fur that traps a layer of water next to their skin.

creatures.name: Xulgath Warrior
creatures.description: Xulgath warriors strike with fury and eager cruelty, always ready for the next fight.

creatures.name: Zoaem
creatures.description: These endlessly curious archons are formed from spinning wheels of golden light given corporeal shape.

creatures.name: Aiuvarin Elementalist
creatures.description: Commonly referred to as half-elves, aiuvarins often have trouble fitting into society. This can lead to many aiuvarins diverting their full attention towards their own personal pursuits, such as studying spellcasting. Elves are mysterious and intelligent, and graceful and cunning in battle.

creatures.name: Animated Armor
creatures.description: A suit of armor animated by magical enchantment.

creatures.name: Boar
creatures.description: A massive, bristly wild boar with dangerous tusks.

creatures.name: Boggard Warrior
creatures.description: Boggard warriors exalt single combat and prefer to fight alone so that no one can contest their kills.

creatures.name: Bugbear Prowler
creatures.description: A hulking, stealthy goblinoid that lurks in the shadows.

creatures.name: Caligni Skulker
creatures.description: Most often found on the surface are caligni skulkers, stealing staple goods and luxuries alike.

creatures.name: Cockroach Swarm
creatures.description: While cockroaches tend to gather in cramped spaces, a disturbed colony is sometimes prone to swarming, where hundreds or even thousands of the insects scurry out of their hiding places in a raft of shiny brown and black carapaces on thousands of skittering legs.

creatures.name: Crocodile
creatures.description: A large reptilian predator lurking in murky waters.

creatures.name: Deinonychus
creatures.description: Deinonychuses are wily hunters that attack in groups of up to a dozen individuals, ripping apart prey with sharp talons and powerful jaws.

creatures.name: Dero Stalker
creatures.description: Dero stalkers explore the surface world by night, seeking out victims to abduct.

creatures.name: Dhampir Wizard
creatures.description: This dhampir is a svetocher, the child of a moroi vampire.

creatures.name: Domovoi
creatures.description: The elderly domovoi are patrons of the home and the dearest of all house spirits to the people they live with.

creatures.name: Draugr
creatures.description: The risen corpses of sailors who died at sea, known as draugrs, reek of rot and decay from the briny deep.

creatures.name: Dromaar Mountaineer
creatures.description: Dromaar mountaineers are hardened half-orc scouts who often lead raiding or scouting parties on dangerous expeditions.

creatures.name: Ember Fox
creatures.description: Ember foxes resemble their canid namesakes, save for the flames that make their fur and the tips of their long whiskers flicker and glow.

creatures.name: Envyspawn
creatures.description: Sinspawn were created by one of seven ancient wizards known collectively as runelords—each of whom embraced and embodied one of seven sins.

creatures.name: Fey Dragonet
creatures.description: Although they are much smaller than their larger dragon cousins, fey dragonets have many of the same physiological attributes, including long necks, toothy maws, sinuous tails, and sharp claws.

creatures.name: Flaming Skull
creatures.description: More dangerous than simple severed heads, these skulls are wreathed in unearthly flames. Beheaded are the reanimated heads of decapitation victims. These mindless undead fly through the air or roll around to attack their prey.

creatures.name: Fungus Leshy
creatures.description: Fungus leshies guard caves, bogs, and damp, dark places.

creatures.name: Ghoul Soldier
creatures.description: Retaining their martial skill, these powerful ghouls are not afraid to meet their foe in the open, feeding on the flesh of their fallen opponents to learn their combat abilities.

creatures.name: Giant Badger
creatures.description: The violent, territorial giant badger is a relentless predator.

creatures.name: Giant Bat
creatures.description: An enormous bat with a terrifying wingspan.

creatures.name: Giant Crab
creatures.description: Crabs are scavenging crustaceans known for their hard shells and iconic sideways gait.

creatures.name: Giant Leech
creatures.description: Capable of growing to lengths of nearly 5 feet, giant leeches have circular maws filled with hook-like teeth.

creatures.name: Giant Monitor Lizard
creatures.description: Large and seemingly ponderous, a monitor lizard is a deceptively swift and ruthless predator.

creatures.name: Giant Opossum
creatures.description: Larger species of opossum can eat nearly anything human-sized or smaller. Few creatures have survived as long and in as many environments as the opossum.

creatures.name: Giant Viper
creatures.description: The giant viper's fangs are a frightening sight, with injection tubes as long as daggers.

creatures.name: Gluttonyspawn
creatures.description: Sinspawn were created by one of seven ancient wizards known collectively as runelords—each of whom embraced and embodied one of seven sins.

creatures.name: Gnoll Hunter
creatures.description: A gnoll hunter with hyena-like features and a savage pack mentality.

creatures.name: Greedspawn
creatures.description: Sinspawn were created by one of seven ancient wizards known collectively as runelords—each of whom embraced and embodied one of seven sins.

creatures.name: Herexen
creatures.description: When a cleric rebels against their deity and dies in the grip of blasphemous rage, the heresies they committed in life may fuel their transformation into an undead herexen.

creatures.name: Hippogriff
creatures.description: With the proud bearing of a great raptor and the magnificence of a powerful horse, hippogriffs are thought to be an accidental fusion of creatures or perhaps the creation of a flesh-warping wizard with a keen aesthetic sense.

creatures.name: Hryngar Taskmaster
creatures.description: Hryngar priests of the taskmaster god Droskar often assume leadership roles within their communities, advancing the goals of their deity through coercion and displays of force.

creatures.name: Icicle Snake
creatures.description: Translucent and capable of hanging suspended and near motionless, icicle snakes sense the heat of living creatures as a threat and attempt to use their camouflage and chilling bite against foes.

creatures.name: Incutilis
creatures.description: Incutilises are intelligent, nautilus-like sea creatures that attack their victims' brains to take over their bodies, creating puppets to use for labor, combat, or their own malevolent amusement.

creatures.name: Jotunborn Sage
creatures.description: Jotunborn are large humanoids with indirect connections to ancient giants like titans.

creatures.name: Kappa
creatures.description: Mischievous by nature, kappas delight in playing pranks on unsuspecting travelers.

creatures.name: Kholo Hunter
creatures.description: Kholo are tall, hyena-headed humanoids who dwell in savannas, warm grasslands, and arid hills.

creatures.name: Kitsune Trickster
creatures.description: With minor magic and shapeshifting capabilities, these tricksters love to play mostly harmless pranks for their own enjoyment.

creatures.name: Kobold Cavern Mage
creatures.description: Kobold cavern mages are born more than trained, hatching from eggs that absorbed particularly large amounts of primal earth energy.

creatures.name: Leprechaun
creatures.description: Leprechauns are mostly jovial tricksters who prefer mischief over conflict.

creatures.name: Living Boulder
creatures.description: Living boulders roll and glide through the Plane of Earth, gathering gemstones and metal shards until their surfaces resemble a ship's hull covered in barnacles.

creatures.name: Lizardfolk Stargazer
creatures.description: The tradition of astrology and looking to the stars for both navigation and prognostication is well-established in iruxi society, and their wise and observant stargazers are among the people's most respected members.

creatures.name: Lustspawn
creatures.description: Sinspawn were created by one of seven ancient wizards known collectively as runelords—each of whom embraced and embodied one of seven sins.

creatures.name: Merfolk Wavecaller
creatures.description: Merfolk wavecallers use their primal magic to defend their people.

creatures.name: Morlock Tinkerer
creatures.description: Most morlocks have little talent for crafting, but many have an uncanny knack for tinkering.

creatures.name: Munavri Spellblade
creatures.description: Although the subterranean Darklands are known for the cruel and domineering civilizations led by fiend-worshipping peoples who dwell within those sinister caverns, not every such subterranean society is ruled that way.

creatures.name: Munsahir
creatures.description: Munsahirs are an elemental people living on the Plane of Fire, often crudely described by mortals as brassy dwarves.

creatures.name: Nagaji Soldier
creatures.description: Nagaji are lithe but muscular bipeds with humanoid figures and serpentine heads.

creatures.name: Orc Commander
creatures.description: An orc who has risen to lead war parties through strength and cunning.

creatures.name: Pipefox
creatures.description: Pipefoxes are small, fluffy snakes with the head of a fox.

creatures.name: Pridespawn
creatures.description: Sinspawn were created by one of seven ancient wizards known collectively as runelords—each of whom embraced and embodied one of seven sins.

creatures.name: Pteranodon
creatures.description: Pteranodons are quick and agile reptiles with 20-foot wingspans that enable them to hover on wind currents for hours.

creatures.name: Pusk
creatures.description: Among the lowest and least powerful of all demons, the wretched pusks are just as vicious and cruel as their more powerful brethren.

creatures.name: Sedacthy Scout
creatures.description: Scouts, usually young sedacthies, ply the ocean in search of animal servants or tread ashore to hunt.

creatures.name: Skeletal Champion
creatures.description: A skeleton warrior with fragments of martial skill from its past life.

creatures.name: Skeletal Horse
creatures.description: Skeletal horses are sometimes used as mounts by other undead or monsters. Animated skeletons are among the most common types of undead.

creatures.name: Slothspawn
creatures.description: Sinspawn were created by one of seven ancient wizards known collectively as runelords—each of whom embraced and embodied one of seven sins.

creatures.name: Slurk
creatures.description: The slurk is a sticky, tusked frog-beast found in underground lairs and caves.

creatures.name: Soulbound Doll (Brave)
creatures.description: Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul.

creatures.name: Soulbound Doll (Calm)
creatures.description: Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul.

creatures.name: Soulbound Doll (Careful)
creatures.description: Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul.

creatures.name: Soulbound Doll (Cruel)
creatures.description: Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul.

creatures.name: Soulbound Doll (Gentle)
creatures.description: Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul.

creatures.name: Soulbound Doll (Impish)
creatures.description: Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul.

creatures.name: Soulbound Doll (Jolly)
creatures.description: Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul.

creatures.name: Soulbound Doll (Kind)
creatures.description: Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul.

creatures.name: Soulbound Doll (Rash)
creatures.description: Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul.

creatures.name: Soulbound Doll (Sassy)
creatures.description: Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul.

creatures.name: Soulbound Doll (Timid)
creatures.description: Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul.

creatures.name: Tengu Sneak
creatures.description: Tengu are an adaptable people originally from the continent of Tian Xia, but whose travels have taken them across all of Golarion.

creatures.name: Umbral Gnome Warrior
creatures.description: Umbral gnome warriors are quick to charge into battle but focus on defending their kin and their homes over more aggressive tactics when a choice is available.

creatures.name: War Horse
creatures.description: A trained warhorse bred for battle.

creatures.name: Warg
creatures.description: An intelligent, wolf-like beast spoken in dark tongues.

creatures.name: Wererat
creatures.description: Wererats tend to be selfishly opportunistic, avaricious, and paranoid as a result of their curse.

creatures.name: Wrathspawn
creatures.description: Sinspawn were created by one of seven ancient wizards known collectively as runelords—each of whom embraced and embodied one of seven sins.

creatures.name: Xulgath Skulker
creatures.description: Xulgath warrens are patrolled—some might say "haunted"—by the community's skulkers.

creatures.name: Zombie Brute
creatures.description: A massive, bloated zombie that relies on raw strength.

creatures.name: Zyss Serpentfolk
creatures.description: Even the least among zyss serpentfolk consider themselves greater than any mammal.

creatures.name: Aapoph Serpentfolk
creatures.description: Aapophs possess greater strength and stronger venom than their zyss kin, but they lack zyss's innate magic.

creatures.name: Akizendri
creatures.description: Akizendris gnaw at sources of knowledge and lore, gleefully corrupting and altering them to vex scholars across the planes with contradictions and untruths.

creatures.name: Animated Statue
creatures.description: Animated statues are often used to guard crypts, small shrines, or areas in government buildings where they can be positioned amid normal statues to hide their true nature until an intruder arouses their ire.

creatures.name: Ankheg
creatures.description: A horse-sized burrowing insect with acid-spitting mandibles.

creatures.name: Ankhrav
creatures.description: These horse-sized, burrowing monsters generally avoid heavily settled areas like cities, but ankhravs' predilection for livestock and humanoid flesh ensures that the creatures do not remain in the deep wilderness for long.

creatures.name: Athamaru Hunter
creatures.description: Deep in the sea, schools of athamarus—piscine humanoids armed with spears and specialized crossbows—stalk sharks, sea serpents, and giant squid from the backs of their giant moray eel mounts.

creatures.name: Boggard Swampseer
creatures.description: The boggard swampseer has been gifted with magic through their worship of the demon lord Gogunta, and they use their power to rule a boggard village, keeping the rest of the village in line and planning raids on nearby communities.

creatures.name: Brine Shark
creatures.description: Brine sharks are deadly elementals that roam the endless oceans of the Plane of Water.

creatures.name: Bugbear Thug
creatures.description: A muscular bugbear bully who uses brute force to get its way.

creatures.name: Bugbear Tormentor
creatures.description: The bugbear tormentor seeks to torture their prey as much through psychological intimidation as through physical harm.

creatures.name: Centaur Herbalist
creatures.description: Many centaurs are skilled in the study of plants, especially those in the areas in which they roam.

creatures.name: Centipede Swarm
creatures.description: Swarms of centipedes are dangerous indeed, ravenous carpets of skittering hunger capable of devouring a traveler whole in a matter of minutes.

creatures.name: Changeling Exile
creatures.description: This changeling exile is the child of a cuckoo hag.

creatures.name: Chupacabra
creatures.description: These notorious predators have an undeniable thirst for blood.

creatures.name: Cinder Rat
creatures.description: These oversized rodents are made of smoldering charcoal and elemental fire, and noxious fumes continually bellow from their flaming flesh.

creatures.name: Cockatrice
creatures.description: A chicken-sized reptilian bird whose bite slowly turns flesh to stone.

creatures.name: Compsognathus Swarm
creatures.description: Though a curious lone compsognathus is occasionally known to sneak into an adventurer's camp, they're often scouting from a nearby pack potentially containing dozens of the tiny bipedal dinosaurs.

creatures.name: D'ziriak
creatures.description: These strange creatures are native to the Netherworld, where their colorful nature is in opposition to that realm's overwhelmingly monochromatic palette.

creatures.name: Dero Strangler
creatures.description: Dero stranglers specialize in capturing living victims, and they are often called upon to aid in abductions.

creatures.name: Dire Wolf
creatures.description: A massive wolf nearly the size of a horse.

creatures.name: Divoynik
creatures.description: A close-knit settlement is rocked to its core when a beloved pillar of the community is accused of a heinous crime.

creatures.name: Dragonblood Occultist
creatures.description: The dragonblood occultist draws power from their omen dragon benefactor.

creatures.name: Draxie
creatures.description: The mischievous dragon sprites called draxies have dueled their pixie cousins for the title of ultimate prankster for centuries.

creatures.name: Drow Priestess
creatures.description: A drow priestess devoted to dark powers.

creatures.name: Dryad
creatures.description: Dryads are fey guardians of the trees and creatures who dwell in wooded areas.

creatures.name: Dvorovoi
creatures.description: Traditionally, the dvorovoi is seen as the domovoi's younger sibling and has charge of a peasant's yard and all the animals and tools within.

creatures.name: Elf Wizard
creatures.description: An elf wizard trained at an arcane academy.

creatures.name: Esobok
creatures.description: Esoboks are brute hunters and pugnacious sentinels who serve as the guard dogs of the Boneyard.

creatures.name: Fen Mosquito Swarm
creatures.description: When deadly fen mosquitoes gather in large numbers, they form into lethal swarms capable of draining blood at a truly alarming rate.

creatures.name: Ganzi Martial Artist
creatures.description: Children of primeval chaos, ganzis intertwine the churning pandemonium of the Maelstrom with the more mundane tumult of mortal life.

creatures.name: Gelatinous Cube
creatures.description: A translucent cubic ooze that engulfs and dissolves prey.

creatures.name: Giant Chameleon
creatures.description: Giant chameleons are legendary for their ability to change their skin color in response to their surroundings. Their eyes are capable of peering in different directions independently, making them almost as difficult to sneak up on as they are to notice in the first place.

creatures.name: Giant Constrictor Snake
creatures.description: An enormous constrictor snake that squeezes prey to death.

creatures.name: Giant Dragonfly Nymph
creatures.description: The aquatic, wingless offspring of dragonflies are called dragonfly nymphs.

creatures.name: Giant Eagle
creatures.description: No mere animals, giant eagles have a keen intellect and a strong sense of honor.

creatures.name: Giant Mantis
creatures.description: These massive cousins of praying mantises stand taller than an average human. These predators possess lightning-quick forelegs and a bone-breaking bite.

creatures.name: Giant Scorpion
creatures.description: A horse-sized scorpion with venomous stinger.

creatures.name: Giant Wasp
creatures.description: A wasp the size of a large dog with a venomous stinger.

creatures.name: Gorilla
creatures.description: Gorillas can be territorial creatures, particularly if provoked by hunters or the presence of more dangerous monsters.

creatures.name: Grioth Cultist
creatures.description: Grioth cultists represent the lowest tier of their remorseless cult-worshippers of the Elder Mythos tasked with leading scouting missions into new worlds.

creatures.name: Grizzly Bear
creatures.description: An enormous brown bear with devastating claws.

creatures.name: Grothlut
creatures.description: Sluglike abominations, grothluts are grotesque dregs of the fleshwarping process.

creatures.name: Hell Hound
creatures.description: A hell hound's appearance dismisses any doubts as to their infernal origins—flesh the color of burning pitch, teeth as sharp as any fiend's pitchfork, and a shroud of ever-burning hellfire are all trademark features.

creatures.name: Hellhound
creatures.description: A hound wreathed in hellfire, summoned from the lower planes.

creatures.name: Hound Topiary
creatures.description: A majestic hound can be found guarding the entrance to several noble gardens across Avistan, often as a warning sign to raiding goblin parties or a devotional plea for protection from Cayden Cailean or Dispater, depending on the region.

creatures.name: Human Cleric
creatures.description: A devout human cleric serving a temple.

creatures.name: Hyaenodon
creatures.description: Hyaenodons are ferocious primeval creatures that resemble hyenas.

creatures.name: Kholo Bonekeeper
creatures.description: Some kholos serve as conduits to the divine and are responsible for placating spirits and gods alike.

creatures.name: Kuribu
creatures.description: Kuribus serve as the guardians of temples and other holy sites.

creatures.name: Lacridaemon
creatures.description: Souls who die from neglect or exposure due to becoming lost, trapped in small spaces, or otherwise being far from help can transform into lacridaemons.

creatures.name: Lion
creatures.description: A majestic predator of the savanna.

creatures.name: Moose
creatures.description: Moose are a large, solitary type of deer. They're majestic, territorial, and extremely grumpy-traits that make them dangerous to unwary travelers and would-be predators alike. Males are instantly recognizable, standing 7 feet tall at the shoulder, with large heads and thick necks to support their i…

creatures.name: Ogre Warrior
creatures.description: A towering, brutish ogre wielding a heavy ogre hook.

creatures.name: Pachycephalosaurus
creatures.description: Pachycephalosauruses are normally peaceful, herbivorous dinosaurs, but they grow much more violent during mating season, when they fight one another to win over mates and warn away interlopers.

creatures.name: Pegasus
creatures.description: The pegasus is a winged horse prized for its capacity to serve as an aerial mount.

creatures.name: Pitborn Adept
creatures.description: One of the most common types of nephilim is the pitborn, who bear a demonic corruption infesting their mortal bloodline.

creatures.name: River Drake
creatures.description: Although the glistening scales and sleek, fin-like wings on these drakes give them an appearance reminiscent of river fish, they are actually distant relatives of the dragons that rule the oceans.

creatures.name: Rust Monster
creatures.description: An insectoid that feeds on metal, corroding weapons and armor on contact.

creatures.name: Sea Hag
creatures.description: Sea hags specialize in transformation magic, preying on those who are desperate to change some aspect of their physical appearance.

creatures.name: Skeletal Giant
creatures.description: The reanimated bones of giants make excellent necromantic thralls. Animated skeletons are among the most common types of undead.

creatures.name: Snapping Flytrap
creatures.description: Snapping flytraps typically have two sets of tooth-edged leaves, each measuring 3 feet wide, at the end of 10-foot-long stalks. Flytraps eagerly feed on humanoids, monstrous insects, and larger prey.

creatures.name: Sod Hound
creatures.description: Sod hounds are mossy extraplanar canines formed of packed dirt and pebbles.

creatures.name: Stirge Swarm
creatures.description: A swarm of blood-sucking stirges that descends in a buzzing cloud.

creatures.name: String Slime
creatures.description: Found underground or in dungeons, these quivering, slug-like ropes of slime continuously scour their domain for food.

creatures.name: Tooth Fairy Swarm
creatures.description: A mob of tooth fairies working together can conduct forced dentistry in seconds.

creatures.name: Twigjack
creatures.description: "True" twigjacks, as they consider themselves, are found in wild forests alongside the fey they idolize.

creatures.name: Unicorn
creatures.description: Symbols of grace and purity, unicorns resemble proud and noble horses.

creatures.name: Veteran Guard
creatures.description: An experienced city guard who has seen real combat.

creatures.name: Werewolf
creatures.description: The curse of the werewolf—known as lycanthropy to many—instills in its carriers the hungry bloodlust and predatory instincts of the wolf.

creatures.name: Wight
creatures.description: An intelligent undead driven by a burning hatred of the living.

creatures.name: Winged Chupacabra
creatures.description: Some chupacabras are mutants with large reptilian wings and have been known to carry off goats or even children.

creatures.name: Xulgath Leader
creatures.description: Xulgath leaders are usually the strongest, most violent members of a community—although in some cases, particularly in larger groups, xulgath clutches are led by powerful demon cultists or other monsters who seek to use the xulgaths as servants or sacrifices.

creatures.name: Zephyr Hawk
creatures.description: Zephyr hawks drift among the currents of the Plane of Air in great flocks.

creatures.name: Abrikandilu
creatures.description: Wrecker demons, also known as abrikandilus, despise beautiful things and do everything in their power to destroy both people and objects regarded as such.

creatures.name: Aghash
creatures.description: Walking embodiments of curses, misfortune, and the evil eye, aghashes wander the deserts of the Material Plane, searching out the arrogant, charming, and persuasive to humiliate and undermine.

creatures.name: Alce
creatures.description: Wingless griffons, known as alces, result from a rare mutation.

creatures.name: Amphisbaena
creatures.description: An amphisbaena is an exceedingly aggressive, venomous viper of remarkable size that bears two heads-one on each end of its body.

creatures.name: Aphorite Sharpshooter
creatures.description: The denizens of the Eternal City of Axis first forged aphorites to serve as emissaries, agents, and facilitators, with one foot in the sublime, perfect order, and one in the messy, murky, confusing muck of the Universe.

creatures.name: Arboreal Warden
creatures.description: Arboreal wardens are the rangers of arboreal society.

creatures.name: Attic Whisperer
creatures.description: Beware the haunting sobs of the attic whisperer, for they carry the pained wrath of an abandoned child who perished due to the neglect or absence of their caretakers.

creatures.name: Bandit Leader
creatures.description: A cunning bandit who has risen to lead through charisma and brutality.

creatures.name: Barghest
creatures.description: Barghests are canine beasts that take great joy in the hunt, often lurking near humanoid settlements to find prey that can provide a suitable challenge.

creatures.name: Bison
creatures.description: Bison are large bovines with short faces and two horns; they weigh an upwards1 of 2,000 pounds and stand up to 6 feet at the withers.

creatures.name: Brood Leech Swarm
creatures.description: Most smaller species of leeches do not tend to swarm, but brood leeches are prone to gathering in seething, undulant mats of squirming gluttony.

creatures.name: Caligni Hunter
creatures.description: Although caligni hunters are often pressed into leading their skulker kin, most hunters prefer to wander the Darklands or raid the surface free from those responsibilities.

creatures.name: Coil Spy
creatures.description: Some serpentfolk undergo intense ritual training and practice to improve their innate ability to disguise themselves.

creatures.name: Daeodon
creatures.description: Where the typical boar is merely ill-tempered and generally unfriendly, the towering daeodon is legitimately hateful and ruthlessly violent.

creatures.name: Displacer Beast
creatures.description: A panther-like beast with long tentacles that projects illusory duplicates.

creatures.name: Duskwalker Ghost Hunter
creatures.description: Duskwalkers are infused with the same energies as psychopomps.

creatures.name: Dwarf Stonecaster
creatures.description: All dwarves share a connection to the earth, but stonecasters have focused and polished that connection.

creatures.name: Earthen Destrier
creatures.description: This wave of dirt takes the crude likeness of the melded forequarters of a charging warhorse and a rocky knight wielding a rudimentary lance of gray stone. Certain earth elementals manifest as specific types of material, be they boulders, sand, or crystals.

creatures.name: Filth Fire
creatures.description: These creatures look like roiling clouds of black smoke churning above a mound of burning refuse.

creatures.name: Gancanagh
creatures.description: Gancanaghs are lovers, revelers, and dashing duelists of Elysium.

creatures.name: Gargoyle
creatures.description: A stone creature disguised among architecture, swooping to attack.

creatures.name: Ghost Commoner
creatures.description: The ghost commoner is an ordinary person who believes they died unjustly. When some mortals die through tragic circumstances or without closure, their souls can linger and haunt a locale significant to them in life.

creatures.name: Giant Dragonfly
creatures.description: These buzzing insects are the size of a small horse.

creatures.name: Giant Stag Beetle
creatures.description: The giant stag beetle is larger than a horse, and the sight of one flying (if slowly and somewhat clumsily) on great buzzing wings is unnerving.

creatures.name: Gigantopithecus
creatures.description: These fierce kin of orangutans are three times heavier than a gorilla. While many apes exhibit peaceful or reclusive behavior, gorillas can be territorial, and the megaprimatus is especially aggressive and dangerous.

creatures.name: Gobmob Snake
creatures.description: These bizarre mutant goblin snakes possess no fewer than three heads. A goblin snake is a foul-smelling serpentine creature covered in greasy black scales and sporting a fanged, lipless head resembling that of a goblin.

creatures.name: Great White Shark
creatures.description: An apex predator among the coastal surface waters where it hunts, the great white shark is one of the largest shark species.

creatures.name: Green Hag
creatures.description: A hideous crone who uses illusions and deception to lure prey.

creatures.name: Griffon
creatures.description: Griffons are regal beasts revered as symbols of freedom and strength in many cultures.

creatures.name: Hadrosaurid
creatures.description: Hadrosaurids are a broad grouping of herbivorous dinosaurs that share characteristic flat snouts filled with rows of grinding teeth well suited for feeding on vegetation.

creatures.name: Hellbound Attorney
creatures.description: The Hellbound attorney sought the assistance of a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Phistophilus]-one of Hell's contract devils-to bolster her legalistic ability on the Universe.

creatures.name: Hermit Crab Swarm
creatures.description: When hermit crabs find a shell that is too large, they lie in wait for others to arrive and move into the shell, abandoning a possible replacement.

creatures.name: Hobgoblin Archer
creatures.description: Bands of hobgoblin soldiers typically have at least one archer among their ranks.

creatures.name: Ice Troll
creatures.description: Ice trolls are nightmarish frozen monsters of rime-stained claws and hoarfrost teeth who haunt glaciers, mountains, and tundras.

creatures.name: Jann
creatures.description: All six elements build each jann and fill them with a constant desire to travel, typically jaunting about the Universe.

creatures.name: Kelpie
creatures.description: Kelpies are malevolent, amphibious fey shapechangers who lure mortals to a watery grave.

creatures.name: Kholo Sergeant
creatures.description: When kholos band together to form mercenary bands or raiding parties, the strongest or most respected among them is often designated the hunt leader or sergeant.

creatures.name: Larval Ofalth
creatures.description: Ofalths reproduce asexually. When they first hatch from their leathery eggs, larval ofalths resemble a tendril of flesh supported by spindly legs, but they soon cocoon themselves in trash, to serve as both armor and camouflage. Found in castle dung heaps, city dumps, and sewers, ofalths are living …

creatures.name: Living Thunderclap
creatures.description: A living thunderclap is a small storm cloud (sometimes in the vague shape of a humanoid) that cracks and booms with thunder. Some elementals embody aspects of air, such as smoke, lightning, and fog.

creatures.name: Lovelorn
creatures.description: A particularly macabre form of undead, these spiderlike creatures resemble still-beating hearts with rib bones for legs and tendrils of gore dangling beneath.

creatures.name: Mandragora
creatures.description: A mandragora looks like a freshly pulled tuber that has grown into the malformed shape of a child with a grotesque face and hideously bloated body.

creatures.name: Mimic
creatures.description: A shape-shifting aberration that disguises itself as mundane objects.

creatures.name: Minotaur
creatures.description: A massive bull-headed humanoid lurking in twisting passages.

creatures.name: Minotaur Hunter
creatures.description: A minotaur is a large humanoid with bovine features such as horns, hooves, and a layer of hair that covers their entire body.

creatures.name: Mist Stalker
creatures.description: The tentacled mist stalker shrouds itself in a cloak of mist through which its single, never-blinking eye can see with clarity, allowing it an advantage when stalking prey. Water elementals that become infused with cold or mist have increased mobility in regions outside of bodies of water.

creatures.name: Myceloid
creatures.description: The ambulatory fungus creatures called myceloids are notorious for spreading deadly purple pox, controlling creatures' minds, and devouring humanoid flesh.

creatures.name: Nightgaunt
creatures.description: Nightgaunts lurk in dreams, lying in wait to spirit away the unwary dreamer.

creatures.name: Ogre Glutton
creatures.description: An ogre consumed by an insatiable hunger, devouring anything in its path.

creatures.name: Owlbear
creatures.description: A feathered bear with the head of an owl, territorial and aggressive.

creatures.name: Phantom Knight
creatures.description: Cavaliers and knights who died for their cause make for particularly strongwilled phantoms.

creatures.name: Pixie
creatures.description: A tiny winged fey creature brimming with magical mischief.

creatures.name: Poracha
creatures.description: Porachas are feline beasts native to the mystical Forest of Spirits in Tian Xia.

creatures.name: Qarna
creatures.description: Qarnas are secretive and tireless sentinels and scouts, patrolling the Outer Sphere's untamed wilds for evil to eliminate and keeping small communities safe from demons, devils, and worse.

creatures.name: Ratfolk Grenadier
creatures.description: Grenadiers use alchemical techniques and stealth to defend their communities.

creatures.name: Rhinoceros
creatures.description: Rhinoceroses are short-tempered, territorial, and easily startled, and these traits combined with their innate ferocity means their natural instinct when disturbed is to attack.

creatures.name: Satyr
creatures.description: A mischievous goat-legged fey creature.

creatures.name: Scarecrow
creatures.description: A ramshackle collection of materials in a human shape, the scarecrow construct is indistinguishable from a normal scarecrow until it slowly creaks to life.

creatures.name: Scorpion Swarm
creatures.description: These massive, terrifying arachnids are typically 8 feet long from head to the base of the tail.

creatures.name: Sedacthy Marauder
creatures.description: Physically imposing sedacthies prove their status by controlling dangerous aquatic creatures like great white sharks and giant moray eels.

creatures.name: Shadow
creatures.description: An incorporeal undead creature formed from darkness itself.

creatures.name: Shadow Spawn
creatures.description: The mysterious undead known as shadows lurk in dark places and feed on those who stray too far from the light.

creatures.name: Sweet Hag
creatures.description: Sweet hags use bright colors, pleasant visages, and sugary treats to draw in children and the young at heart.

creatures.name: Tiger
creatures.description: Tigers are solitary and territorial hunters, using their striped hides to blend into the forests and jungles they call home and preferring to attack with surprise.

creatures.name: Ugothol
creatures.description: Among the subtler of the alghollthu creations were the ugothols—also known as faceless stalkers.

creatures.name: Vampire Servitor
creatures.description: Vampires use their servitor pawns for infiltration and reconnaissance. Vampires are undead creatures that feed on the blood of the living.

creatures.name: Vampire Spawn
creatures.description: A pale, undead creature bound to the will of a master vampire.

creatures.name: Wasp Swarm
creatures.description: Wasp nests are made of chewed wood fibers foraged from the surrounding flora, which the wasps make into a material similar to paper.

creatures.name: Werebear
creatures.description: Unwavering conviction fills a werebear during their transformations.

creatures.name: Weretiger
creatures.description: These ferocious werecreatures stalk prey with the cunning and skill of a true apex predator.

creatures.name: Air Elemental
creatures.description: A swirling vortex of wind in a barely-visible humanoid shape.

creatures.name: Army Ant Swarm
creatures.description: An army ant swarm is a terrifying carpet of stinging insects that devours all in its path. Ants are industrious insects that aid the natural processes of decay and renewal.

creatures.name: Azuretzi
creatures.description: Azuretzis are sinuous, serpentine creatures with daggersharp teeth covered in brilliant blue scales and mottled purple and pink highlights that shimmer in a pareidolic approximation of leering, laughing faces.

creatures.name: Basilisk
creatures.description: A reptilian beast whose gaze turns creatures to stone.

creatures.name: Bogwid
creatures.description: The abhorrent combination between a toad and an octopus, a bogwid drags its bloated green body through the swamps in search of a meal for the many larvae it carries on its back.

creatures.name: Brimorak
creatures.description: These goat-headed demons have glowing red eyes and flaming hooves.

creatures.name: Caligni Vanguard
creatures.description: Caligni children born without eyes-an extremely rare occurrence-are considered sacred to their communities.

creatures.name: Catrina
creatures.description: Not all spirits who enter the Boneyard realize they have died.

creatures.name: Cyclops
creatures.description: The kingdoms of the cyclopes date to an age before the rise of humanity, when dragons and giants and serpentfolk ruled the world.

creatures.name: Dero Magister
creatures.description: Dero magisters are leaders among dero society.

creatures.name: Dig-Widget
creatures.description: Thieves covet dig-widgets, specialized constructs built for infiltration.

creatures.name: Earth Elemental
creatures.description: A hulking mass of rock and stone in humanoid form.

creatures.name: Emperor Cobra
creatures.description: These powerful serpents infest bogs and lowlands.

creatures.name: Ether Spider
creatures.description: Ether spiders are members of a vague taxonomy called ethereal wildlife and are deadly predators from the Ethereal Plane.

creatures.name: Fire Elemental
creatures.description: A roiling column of flame in a vaguely humanoid shape.

creatures.name: Flame Drake
creatures.description: Flame drakes dwell near volcanoes and magma, but it's not unheard of for one to drift into nearby areas like forests or wooded hills.

creatures.name: Forest Troll
creatures.description: Forest trolls are gangly giants who stalk the fringes of civilization.

creatures.name: Giant Crawling Hand
creatures.description: A giant crawling hand is the appendage of a very large creature, such as a giant.

creatures.name: Giant Frilled Lizard
creatures.description: Found in warm and tropical forests and savannas, these immense lizards are a deadly threat to the unsuspecting traveler.

creatures.name: Giant Moray Eel
creatures.description: Giant moray eels dwell in warm, tropical waters and build lairs in narrow, twisting caves made of coral.

creatures.name: Gladiator
creatures.description: A seasoned arena combatant who fights for glory and gold.

creatures.name: Globster
creatures.description: The tide washes ashore all manner of detritus, from harmless seaweed and shells to the rotting corpses of massive aquatic creatures.

creatures.name: Gnokesh
creatures.description: The structure of a gnokesh, a wheel of ancient tomes, refects its role in Heaven.

creatures.name: Grodair
creatures.description: Grodairs are bizarre fishlike creature native to the First World.

creatures.name: Harpy
creatures.description: A winged humanoid whose enchanting song lures prey to their doom.

creatures.name: Hippopotamus
creatures.description: Typical adult hippos move quickly on land and attack stealthily in the water. Hippopotamuses, or hippos for short, are semiaquatic animals that spend most of their time in rivers and lakes, but can thrive on land.

creatures.name: Jaathoom
creatures.description: Possessing all the subtlety and elegance of air itself, the jaathooms of the Plane of Air operate within dreams, nightmares, and time.

creatures.name: Kodama
creatures.description: A kodama is a type of kami who inhabits a tree.

creatures.name: Lampad
creatures.description: Lampads protect dark, hidden places underground.

creatures.name: Lawbringer Warpriest
creatures.description: Mortals whose ancestry has been influenced by archons are called lawbringers.

creatures.name: Living Landslide
creatures.description: Living landslides resemble humanoids made of earth and gravel.

creatures.name: Living Waterfall
creatures.description: Living waterfalls are humanoid-shaped columns of churning water.

creatures.name: Living Whirlwind
creatures.description: A living whirlwind resembles a dust devil with a vague mouth and eyes formed among the dust and debris swirling within it.

creatures.name: Living Wildfire
creatures.description: Living wildfires appear as humanoids made of living fire, burning everything they come across for the pure joy of it.

creatures.name: Necromancer
creatures.description: A dark wizard specializing in the magic of death and undeath.

creatures.name: Orca
creatures.description: While many know orcas as "killer whales," they're actually the largest species of dolphin.

creatures.name: Phase Spider
creatures.description: An ethereal spider that phases in and out of the material plane.

creatures.name: Poltergeist
creatures.description: When a creature dies, and for whatever reason its spirit is unable or unwilling to leave the site of its death, that spirit may manifest as a poltergeist: a restless, invisible spirit that is still able to manipulate physical objects.

creatures.name: Redcap
creatures.description: Redcaps are sadistic and capricious fey who thrill in bloodletting and murder.

creatures.name: Tomb Jelly
creatures.description: Tomb jellies are animate masses of protoplasm with a sickly combination of yellow, gray, and black hues.

creatures.name: Troll
creatures.description: A lanky, green-skinned giant that regenerates from all but fire and acid.

creatures.name: Umbral Gnome Rockwarden
creatures.description: Umbral gnome rockwardens follow druidic teachings and commune with the natural elemental influences and denizens of the Darklands.

creatures.name: Venedaemon
creatures.description: Those killed by magic or killed in the pursuit of magic can arise as venedaemons.

creatures.name: Vescavor Swarm
creatures.description: The most common and numerous vescavors are the raving swarms.

creatures.name: Vordine
creatures.description: The vast city of Dis trains endless legions of vordines to guard the upper layers of Hell and march across the planes at the archdevils' orders.

creatures.name: Water Elemental
creatures.description: A churning mass of water in humanoid form.

creatures.name: Witchwarg
creatures.description: Witchwargs are related to wargs, but are larger, smarter, and far more dangerous.

creatures.name: Yeti
creatures.description: Nearly a myth, a yeti is rarely seen—and even when it is, it is often too late.

creatures.name: Aapoph Granitescale
creatures.description: The mutated aapophs dubbed granitescales have bulky frames covered in hard gray plates.

creatures.name: Ahuizotl
creatures.description: The ahuizotl is a vicious, semiaquatic predator that resembles a hideous cross between a badger and an otter, with disturbingly web-fngered paws supplemented by a ffth hand at the end of a long, serpentine tail.

creatures.name: Ankylosaurus
creatures.description: Squat, heavily armored quadrupeds, ankylosauruses are stubborn and irascible.

creatures.name: Awakened Tree
creatures.description: Arboreal regents and other wielders of powerful primal magic grant temporary sentience to trees in order to protect the forest.

creatures.name: Beetle Carapace
creatures.description: Created from the exoskeleton of a giant stag beetle, this mindless husk can cut a foe in half using its powerful mandibles.

creatures.name: Blizzardborn
creatures.description: Freezing and thawing in a near-constant loop, blizzardborn resemble humanoid forms composed of a mixture of partially melted snow and sleet.

creatures.name: Bodach
creatures.description: Creature imported from Foundry PF2e data.

creatures.name: Caligni Caller
creatures.description: Although the mysterious demigods known as the Forsaken disappeared eons ago, many calignis continue to follow their ancient traditions of worship.

creatures.name: Cave Bear
creatures.description: Larger, stronger, and far more aggressive than its smaller cousins, the cave bear is a behemoth that avoids civilized lands, preferring to dwell in remote places.

creatures.name: Cave Giant
creatures.description: Cave giants are the vicious loners of the giant world.

creatures.name: Cavern Troll
creatures.description: Insatiable scavengers, cavern trolls stalk the eternal gloom of the Darklands, consuming all in their path—even rocks and minerals.

creatures.name: Choral
creatures.description: Choral angels are incredible singers who fill the halls of Nirvana with pious chants and sacred hymns.

creatures.name: Clockwork Soldier
creatures.description: These diligent machines guard their assigned posts tirelessly.

creatures.name: Divine Warden of Pharasma
creatures.description: This divine warden serves Pharasma, the goddess of birth, death, and fate. It protects a temple or shrine to the Lady of Graves.

creatures.name: Elananx
creatures.description: These strange, fey felines resemble large, broad bobcats from a distance, but a closer view reveals something amiss.

creatures.name: Ettin
creatures.description: A two-headed giant, each head arguing with the other.

creatures.name: Evangelist
creatures.description: Evangelists, the velstracs' unofficial ambassadors, roam the farthest reaches of the planes to spread the word of their kind's abhorrent belief in perfection through pain.

creatures.name: Fire Jellyfish Swarm
creatures.description: While individually one of these fist-sized jellyfish is merely a nuisance, in great numbers, fire jellyfish can form into dangerous swarms.

creatures.name: Giant Mosquito
creatures.description: These horrifically enlarged versions of the common mosquito often prey upon megafauna like dinosaurs and other large creatures, but they won't turn down a chance to drink the blood of a smaller target-such as a humanoid.

creatures.name: Giant Tarantula
creatures.description: Tarantulas are ambush predators, but will attack prey in the open. Few everyday vermin inspire as much dread as the infamous spider.

creatures.name: Graveknight Captain
creatures.description: Graveknight captains once proudly led squads of troops in battle. When a fearsome combatant falls in battle, the warrior's vengeful spirit can sometimes fuse with their armor, creating a graveknight.

creatures.name: Hobgoblin General
creatures.description: Hobgoblin generals serve as leaders of entire armies and rulers of hobgoblin settlements.

creatures.name: Hodag
creatures.description: Because they're often fantastically described in the wild tall tales of loggers or miners, hodags are considered by many to exist only in local folklore.

creatures.name: Hungry Ghost
creatures.description: Hungry ghosts arise from those who didn't receive proper burials or whose graves were neglected.

creatures.name: Hydra
creatures.description: A multi-headed serpentine beast that regrows severed heads.

creatures.name: Iguanodon
creatures.description: Iguanodons are large, herbivorous dinosaurs that inhabit swamps and forests where they browse on the abundant vegetation.

creatures.name: Iron Hag
creatures.description: Iron hags are kidnappers, targeting those too young to even remember to fight back.

creatures.name: Jungle Drake
creatures.description: Believed to be related to horned dragons, jungle drakes are dangerous hunters equipped with a debilitating venom delivered by a large barbed stinger or their noxious phlegm.

creatures.name: Lamia
creatures.description: Just as they were cursed long ago, lamias can inflict a curse on those they touch, clouding the victim's mind and pushing conscious thought toward animalistic instincts.

creatures.name: Maftet Guardian
creatures.description: Maftets are hawk-winged humanoids with leonine lower bodies that dwell in ancient ruins and cities thought lost, typically in desert or mountain regions.

creatures.name: Manticore
creatures.description: A lion-bodied beast with a human face, bat wings, and a tail of deadly spikes.

creatures.name: Manticore (Quill Tail)
creatures.description: A manticore is a monstrous amalgamation of lion, dragon, and human with a penchant for feasting on human flesh.

creatures.name: Manticore (Scorpion Tail)
creatures.description: A manticore is a monstrous amalgamation of lion, dragon, and human with a penchant for feasting on human flesh.

creatures.name: Mi-Go
creatures.description: Mi-gos are both scientists and colonists, yet their extraterrestrial nature and malevolent motives color their investigations with cruelty.

creatures.name: Mummy Guardian
creatures.description: An ancient corpse wrapped in funerary bandages, guardian of forgotten tombs.

creatures.name: Nightmare
creatures.description: Nightmares are flaming equine harbingers of death.

creatures.name: Revenant
creatures.description: Revenants are obsessed, undead stalkers who arise from their own murders and are driven by only one thing: revenge against their killers.

creatures.name: Rhu-Chalik
creatures.description: Also called void wanderers, rhu-chaliks can survive the depths of space indefinitely, passing between worlds over the eons and scouting those planets for the Dominion of the Black.

creatures.name: Sargassum Heap
creatures.description: A sargassum heap is a mass of semi-intelligent seaweed that floats through the ocean, luring in its victims with hallucinogenic spores.

creatures.name: Sedacthy Speaker
creatures.description: High-ranking sedacthies are expected to both plan campaigns and wade into battle.

creatures.name: Shambling Mound
creatures.description: A mobile mass of rotting vegetation animated by lightning.

creatures.name: Smilodon
creatures.description: Smilodons are large saber-toothed cats, apex predators that are significantly more muscular and broader than the other species of big cats.

creatures.name: Vampire Count
creatures.description: Vampire counts rule their demesnes and subjects through a mix of fear and cruelty. Vampires are undead creatures that feed on the blood of the living.

creatures.name: Will-o'-Wisp
creatures.description: Malevolent balls of colored light, will-o'-wisps haunt lonely marshes and forests, where they lure unsuspecting travelers into danger.

creatures.name: Woolly Rhinoceros
creatures.description: Even bulkier than their non-woolly cousins, these rhinoceroses have a shaggy pelt of long, thick fur and a huge, crescent-shaped horn.

creatures.name: Wraith
creatures.description: An incorporeal undead spirit that drains the life force of the living.

creatures.name: Wyvern
creatures.description: A two-legged drake with a venomous barbed tail.

creatures.name: Young White Dragon
creatures.description: A young white dragon; least intelligent but most feral of chromatic dragons.

creatures.name: Zecui
creatures.description: These chitinous entities can only grow to adulthood within a host creature, usually a humanoid or larger animal.

creatures.name: Zombie Hulk
creatures.description: These towering horrors rise from monstrous, gigantic corpses. A zombie's only desire is to consume the living. Unthinking and ever-shambling harbingers of death, zombies stop only when they're destroyed.

creatures.name: Aboleth
creatures.description: An ancient aquatic aberration that enslaves minds and warps flesh.

creatures.name: Aesra
creatures.description: Despite their flaming blades and ring of unblinking eyes, aesras are the diplomats of peace among the archons, preferring justice via compromise and mutual benefit rather than justice by the sword.

creatures.name: Allosaurus
creatures.description: The allosaurus is a huge bipedal dinosaur built for hunting larger prey.

creatures.name: Aqudel
creatures.description: Aqudels are obsessed with control. They consider everything around them open to manipulation, and their position in the alghollthu hierarchy leads them to lord over networks of ugothols or small, established societies that they often bully and exploit from the shadows. Aqudels answer only to vidile…

creatures.name: Arboreal Reaper
creatures.description: While some forests have an idyllic, peaceful quality to them, others feel distinctly unfriendly or even sinister-these forests are the favored haunts of arboreal reapers.

creatures.name: Azarketi Tide Tamer
creatures.description: The most ambitious and capable azarketis become tide tamers, learning how to speak with and train aquatic animals.

creatures.name: Black Pudding
creatures.description: A massive black ooze that corrodes metal and flesh alike.

creatures.name: Chuul
creatures.description: A massive lobster-like aberration with paralytic tentacles.

creatures.name: Coarti
creatures.description: The burning-winged coartis are marked by the onerous contracts they agreed to and bound to Hell by the machinations of a legalistic devil, usually a phistophilus.

creatures.name: Con Rit
creatures.description: The con rit is a hulking aquatic centipede that has traded its hundreds of legs for just as many fins.

creatures.name: Drainberry Bush
creatures.description: Drainberry bushes are floating bushes that originate from the First World, with long, thorny vines and dense clusters of bright-red berries.

creatures.name: Dullahan
creatures.description: Riding on a horse as black as night, the headless hunter known as the dullahan tracks down and takes the heads of those they deem unfit to continue living.

creatures.name: Dweomercat
creatures.description: Dweomercats are magically gifted, four-eyed felines from the First World, where they prey upon other creatures and feed on their primal energy.

creatures.name: Elasmosaurus
creatures.description: Elasmosauruses are long-necked, primeval reptiles that dwell in deep oceans and seas.

creatures.name: Elephant
creatures.description: Those who live near elephants have learned to be wary of angering the beasts, but even with precautions in place, elephants sometimes still rampage.

creatures.name: Frost Drake
creatures.description: Frost drakes pose an immense danger in the frozen reaches they call home, where they roam far and wide to hunt for prey such as caribou, wolves, small bears, tundra-dwelling people, and even lone frost giants.

creatures.name: Giant Animated Statue
creatures.description: Giant animated statues' increased size and power make them most useful in large vaults, spacious chambers, or outdoor locations.

creatures.name: Giant Jellyfish
creatures.description: Unlike its smaller cousins, the giant jellyfish is an active predator that chases down its prey through reefs or open water.

creatures.name: Greater Shadow
creatures.description: A more powerful shadow that commands lesser shadows.
